text.title: Readiness Matrix Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Readiness Matrix Template` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every (service × column) cell is filled or explicitly `not_applicable: <reason>`. Empty cells fail review.
rule.rationale: Empty cells are the leading indicator of an unowned incident.

rule.statement: Every service row carries a runbook URL or a `runbook_missing: open-ticket-<id>` placeholder with an assignee.
rule.rationale: Runbook absence is the highest-leverage readiness gap.

rule.statement: Every service has a named human owner + escalation path (not team / channel).
Every service has at least one alert mapped to an SLO/SLI and routed to the owner.
rule.rationale: Unalerted services depend on user complaints.

rule.statement: Every service has a stated DR tier (T0 / T1 / T2) with RTO + RPO. Unstated tier = T2 (lowest).
Output is a versioned snapshot committed to the repo with reviewer signoff and date.
When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
